page: Amazon Web Services c8ib.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance belonging to the c8ib family. It is powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system features 192 virtual CPUs derived from 96 physical cores, utilizing a hyperthreading configuration of 2.0 threads per core.

This instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, which equates to a memory allocation of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

The c8ib.48xlarge is designed for compute-heavy workloads that require dense CPU resources but do not demand local storage or GPU acceleration. The 1:2 ratio of vCPUs to memory makes it configured for CPU-bound tasks, though the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network storage, which may impact I/O-heavy operations. Suitable workloads include HPC, batch processing, distributed analytics, and compute-intensive web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors running at 3.9 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this server features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. It is configured with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance includes no local storage and no GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for compute-intensive tasks such as HPC, batch processing, and distributed analytics, where dense CPU resources are required and network-attached storage is sufficient.
